name: Pu-Xian Chinese

ancestors.notes: Descent per Glottolog classification of Pu-Xian Chinese (puxi1243).

notes: Skeleton. Pu-Xian (Puxian, Xinghua/Hinghwa) is a branch of Min Chinese written in Han (Chinese) characters. Han script is logographic: a character's reading is a lexical fact, not composable from graphic parts, so grapheme-to-IPA requires a per-word pronunciation stratum (a character-to-reading lexicon for this specific variety) that the engine does not bundle. Without that stratum engine coverage is effectively zero, so graphemes are left empty rather than faked. Tone, which Pu-Xian marks lexically and reshapes through extensive tone sandhi, is likewise not recoverable from the characters.

sources.author: Hammarström, H. et al. (Glottolog 5.0)
sources.title: Glottolog: Pu-Xian Chinese (puxi1243)
sources.publisher: Max Planck Institute for Evolutionary Anthropology
